fixture.description: A server-side request is issued to a URL taken straight from user input with no validation — server-side request forgery (SSRF).

grader.rubric: PASS if the report flags that fetchResource issues a server-side request to the user-controlled `target` URL without validation, enabling SSRF (server-side request forgery) — an attacker can make the server reach internal services or cloud metadata endpoints. It should recommend validating against an allowlist and/or blocking internal/loopback/link-local addresses. Severity IMPORTANT or CRITICAL. FAIL if the SSRF risk is not surfaced.

expected_behavior: Security agent flags the unvalidated server-side request to user-controlled URL as SSRF
expected_not_behavior: Does NOT treat it as a generic 'no error handling' note only
Does NOT require proof of a reachable internal host
